# HBM Investment Report — Final Synthesis

**Date:** 2026-05-16 | **Focus:** High Bandwidth Memory competitive landscape & investment positioning

---

## Executive Summary

The HBM market is the defining battleground of the AI semiconductor cycle. At ~$16B in CY2024, the broad-definition TAM is projected to reach $70–100B by CY2028 — a 38%+ CAGR — driven by 4–12 HBM stacks per AI accelerator and a 5× ASP premium over commodity DRAM. Supply is sold out through CY2026, and the three-player oligopoly (SK Hynix ~62%, Micron ~21%, Samsung ~17%) creates structural pricing power.

**Key conviction:** SK Hynix is the highest-quality pure-play on HBM but priced for perfection. Micron offers the best risk/reward with accelerating HBM4 share gains and a clear path to $20B+ HBM revenue by CY2028. Samsung is the wildcard — massive scale but execution risk on HBM4 yields and persistent foundry drag.

---

## Company Comparison Table

| Metric | SK Hynix | Micron (MU) | Samsung (DS) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **CY2025 Revenue** | ~$50B | ~$40B | ~$91B (DS) |
| **HBM Market Share** | ~62% | ~21% | ~17% |
| **HBM Revenue (est. CY25)** | ~$22B | ~$7.5B | ~$6B |
| **Gross Margin** | ~50% | ~38% → 42% (Q4 guide) | ~40% (Q4 memory) |
| **Operating Margin** | ~42% | ~25% → 27% | ~19% (DS div) |
| **HBM Product Lead** | HBM3E 12-layer (dominant) | HBM3E (qualified for GB300) | HBM3E (recovering); HBM4 sampling |
| **CY2028 HBM Rev (Base)** | ~$18.6B | ~$20.4B | ~$17–19B |
| **CY2028 EPS (Base)** | ~₩9,230 ($7.10) | ~$23.61 | ~₩8,200–9,700 ($5.70–6.80) |
| **Key Competitive Edge** | NVIDIA exclusivity; yield leadership | Fastest HBM4 yield ramp; US-based | Scale; vertical integration |
| **Key Risk** | Tariff exposure; share erosion | CapEx intensity; cycle risk | HBM4 yield risk; foundry losses |
| **Probability-Weighted EPS** | ~₩7,800 ($6.00) | ~$21.50 | ~₩8,000 ($5.60) |

## Catalyst Calendar

| Timeframe | Catalyst | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| **H2 2026** | Samsung HBM4 mass production begins | ↑ Samsung share if yields >55%; ↓ if <45% |
| **H2 2026** | Micron HBM4 qualification for Nvidia Vera-Rubin | ↑ Micron share if won; thesis upgrade |
| **Q3-Q4 2026** | SK Hynix HBM4 volume ramp | Consolidates #1 position; margin inflection |
| **2027 H1** | TSMC CoWoS capacity reaches 93kwpm | Removes packaging bottleneck; enables HBM volume |
| **2027** | AI capex cycle inflection (hyperscaler ROI scrutiny) | ↓ All names if capex slows |
| **2027 H2** | DRAM cycle peak risk (HBM capacity displaces commodity) | ↓ Margins across sector |
| **2028** | HBM4 16Hi stacks (48–64GB) enter production | ↑ TAM; favors yield leaders |
| **2028** | TSMC SoW with 20+ HBM stacks | Structural demand expansion |
| **Ongoing** | US tariff policy on Korean/Chinese semiconductors | ↓ SK Hynix & Samsung if >25%; ↑ Micron (US-based) |

---

## Thesis Invalidation Signals

| Signal | Implication | Affected |
|---|---|---|
| **HBM ASP premium compresses below 2× DDR5** | Oligopoly pricing power eroding; shift to bear | All |
| **SK Hynix HBM share drops below 55%** | Competitive moat weakening | SK Hynix ↓ |
| **Samsung HBM4 yield sustains <45%** | Samsung bear case; share shifts to MU/SK | Samsung ↓, MU ↑ |
| **Micron loses Nvidia HBM4 qualification** | Bear case for MU; share loss to SK/Samsung | MU ↓↓ |
| **DRAM oversupply (HBM wafer displacement >25%)** | Commodity margins crash; cycle downturn | All ↓ |
| **AI capex growth slows below 15% YoY** | Demand destruction across HBM chain | All ↓ |
| **US imposes >25% tariff on Korean chips** | SK Hynix & Samsung margin hit 3–5pp | SK/Samsung ↓, MU ↑ |
| **HBM TAM reaches $100B by 2028** | Upside to all scenarios | All ↑ |
| **Samsung foundry turns profitable** | DS margin inflection; bear case off table | Samsung ↑ |
| **Micron HBM share exceeds 28%** | Bull case; faster-than-expected share gains | MU ↑↑ |

## Peer Positioning & Relative Value

| Dimension | SK Hynix | Micron | Samsung |
|---|---|---|---|
| **HBM Quality** | ★★★★★ | ★★★★☆ | ★★★☆☆ |
| **Execution Risk** | Low | Medium | High |
| **Margin Trajectory** | Peak → normalize | Expanding rapidly | Recovering |
| **CapEx Intensity** | Moderate | Very High ($20B FY26) | High (foundry drag) |
| **US Policy Tailwind** | Neutral/Negative | Positive (CHIPS Act, US-based) | Neutral/Negative |
| **Cycle Sensitivity** | High (pure memory) | High (pure memory) | Moderate (diversified) |
| **Valuation (CY28 P/E)** | ~13× (base EPS) | ~23× (base EPS) | ~10× (base EPS) |
| **Risk/Reward Profile** | Quality at a price | Best asymmetric upside | Turnaround optionality |

### Investment Implications

1. **Micron (MU)** — Best risk/reward. HBM sold out through CY2026, HBM4 yield ramp ahead of expectations, and US policy tailwind. Base case EPS of ~$23.61 at ~23× forward P/E leaves room for upside if HBM4 share gains accelerate. Key risk: $20B+ annual CapEx creates FCF pressure in a downturn.

2. **SK Hynix** — Highest quality but priced for perfection at ~13× base EPS. Margin at cycle peak (42% OP). Share erosion below 60% or tariff escalation are the key risks. Best positioned if AI capex supercycle extends through 2028.

3. **Samsung** — Turnaround optionality with the widest scenario spread. HBM4 execution is the binary catalyst. Probability-weighted EPS of ~₩8,000 at ~10× P/E looks cheap, but foundry drag and HBM yield uncertainty warrant a discount. Monitor quarterly HBM yield data closely.

## TAM Reconciliation

| CY2028 HBM TAM Source | Estimate | Definition |
|---|---|---|
| Micron CEO | $100B | Broad (full BOM) |
| TrendForce synthesis | $70–100B | Broad (DRAM share) |
| Yole Group | ~$70B | Broad (full BOM) |
| This analysis (base) | $85B | Broad (weighted) |
| Mordor/Fortune/R&M | $8–12B | Narrow (die-level only) |

> **Note:** The ~4–5× gap between broad and narrow definitions reflects packaging, TSV processing, and test value-add. For semiconductor investment analysis, the broad definition is more relevant.

---

*Report compiled from: Micron Q3 FY2025 earnings, Samsung FY2025 earnings, SK Hynix Q1 2025 earnings, TSMC Q1 2026 earnings, TrendForce, Yole Group, Counterpoint Research, Morgan Stanley, and industry analyst reports.*
